title: pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. lanatum
intro: Pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. lanatum is a plant species belonging to the family Pittosporaceae, native to Ethiopia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. lanatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal tea or tincture.
